$comment: Pinned naga-cli acquisition manifest for the WGSL leg's NATIVE-CONSUMER cross-validation (R-REND-005, issue #133, sub-task D of #119). naga is NOT the wired SPIR-V->WGSL tool — the measured ruling picked Tint (tools/tint-toolchain.json; naga's SPIR-V reader covered only 20/36 of the real corpus — docs/wgsl-tool-decision.md). naga's two roles here: (a) the `shader-crosscompile-roundtrip` ctest validates every Tint-emitted WGSL module with naga too, because the native path consumes WGSL through naga inside the in-tree wgpu-native (spikes/webgpu/FINDINGS.md — emitted WGSL must satisfy BOTH consumers); (b) the re-runnable evaluation harness (tools/wgsl_tool_eval.py) drives it as the Tint alternative. SINGLE source of truth for the pin — parsed by tools/fetch_naga.py (cargo-install + fail-closed `--version` verify). Channel = crates.io FROM-SOURCE via `cargo install --locked` — naga-cli publishes NO official prebuilt binaries, and from-source is the L-42-PREFERRED acquisition anyway (cargo enforces the crates.io registry checksums itself, so this channel needs no SHA-pin sidecar; it is the same posture as the vcpkg manifest feature, not the third-party-prebuilt carve-out). naga is a standalone CLI invoked as a SUBPROCESS, never linked into the engine (the esbuild/tools/ts-toolchain.json precedent). VERSION POLICY: pinned to the same major as the in-tree wgpu-native consumer (v29.0.1.1, spikes/webgpu) so cross-validation runs on the exact naga generation that consumes the WGSL on the native path; bump in lockstep with wgpu-native bumps.
